title: Legacy save migration via word-set compare
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

The launch branch for metadata-less legacy saves — the path that reaches the currently-bouncing cohort, whose save never gets rewritten because they leave before loading anything new. On launch with a legacy save: fetch today's puzzle and set-compare its words against the saved tiles (order-insensitive over normalized uppercase words; accented words like EL NIÑO must compare correctly). Set-equal → it is today's board: keep it in place with progress intact and stamp it (date = today, source = daily). Different → standard swap: old board to the previous slot, today's board loads with the resume notice. Fetch failure/timeout → resume the legacy board untouched, no stamp (retry next launch).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A legacy save whose 16 tiles match today's words in any order resumes with locks/labels intact, and the board header now shows "Today" (stamped).
- [ ] A legacy save with different words swaps to today's board with the resume notice; the old board is recoverable via resume with progress intact.
- [ ] Fetch failure resumes the legacy board untouched and leaves it unstamped, so the next launch retries the comparison.
- [ ] Vitest covers the compare semantics (order-insensitivity, accents, near-miss sets) and all three branch outcomes; npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-4i5 (auto-load + lossless resume)

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Migrate legacy metadata-less saves at launch by fetching today's puzzle and word-set comparing: stamp in place if it is today's board, otherwise swap with the resume notice.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-4i5, boards with `source: 'unknown'` (parsed from the pre-metadata flat blob) resume silently — `decideLaunch` maps them to `resume`. The users this feature exists for are stuck here: their save predates metadata and never gets rewritten, because they open the app, see a stale board, and leave without loading anything.

**Desired behavior:**
`decideLaunch` maps `source: 'unknown'` to a new `fetch-compare` action. The launch flow then shows the loading screen and fetches today's puzzle, and the store decides from the response:

- **Set-equal** — the response's 16 words equal the saved 16 tiles as an unordered set (compare after trimming and uppercasing; words must match exactly otherwise, including accented tiles like "EL NIÑO"; tile order, locks, and labels are irrelevant to the comparison): this is today's board. Keep `current` exactly as it is (tiles/locks/labels untouched) and stamp it `date: <response date>, source: 'daily', chosenExplicitly: false`. No notice. The header now reads "Today" and subsequent launches take the normal dated paths.
- **Different** — stale or foreign board: identical outcome to the `fetch-swap` path in connections-4i5 — old board (still `source: 'unknown'`) moves to `previous`, today's board becomes `current`, resume notice shown. If later resumed, the swap marks it `chosenExplicitly`, exempting it thereafter.
- **Fetch failure/timeout** — resume the legacy board untouched and unstamped, so the next launch retries the comparison. (With connections-apm landed, this failure path shows the banner like any stale-launch failure.)

**Key interfaces:**
- `decideLaunch` — the `unknown` branch changes from `resume` to `fetch-compare`.
- A pure store function applying a daily fetch result to a `fetch-compare` state, returning the next saved state plus whether a swap occurred (the app shows the notice only when it did).
- The word-set comparison — pure, order-insensitive, exact-match-after-normalization semantics as above.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] A legacy save whose tiles match today's words in any order resumes with locks/labels intact, is stamped as today's daily board, and shows "Today" in the header; no notice.
- [ ] A legacy save with different words lands on today's board with the resume notice; resume restores the old board fully.
- [ ] Fetch failure resumes the legacy board untouched and unstamped; the comparison re-runs on the next launch.
- [ ] Vitest covers the comparison (order-insensitivity, accents, near-miss sets differing by one word, duplicate-safe equality) and all three branches. `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating OCR/manual/demo-sourced boards (they have metadata and are exempt by design).
- Any heuristic beyond exact set equality — a near-miss is a different board.

title: Auto-load today's puzzle on a new day with a lossless resume notice
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

The headline behavior. On page load, the pure module decides the launch action: no save → fetch today (existing behavior); current board exempt (chosen explicitly or dateless source) or already today's daily → resume silently; stale non-exempt daily → show the loading screen, fetch today, move the old board to the previous slot, and land on today's board with a dismissible notice (draft copy: "Loaded today's puzzle. [↩ Resume Monday's board] ✕"). Tapping resume swaps current ↔ previous losslessly and marks the re-entered board chosen-explicitly, exempting it from future auto-swaps. The notice is transient — shown at swap time only, not persisted across reloads. Fetch failure/timeout on the stale path falls back to resuming the old board (the dedicated "Today's puzzle is ready" banner for this fallback ships in a later slice).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Returning on a later ET date with a stale auto-loaded daily board lands on today's board with the resume notice visible; the old board is in the previous slot.
- [ ] Tapping resume restores the old board with all locks/labels intact; today's board (including any sorting already done) is preserved and reachable by swapping back; the resumed board does not auto-swap on subsequent launches.
- [ ] Boards loaded via a past-date chip, and OCR/manual/demo boards, resume silently with no notice; a same-day return (board dated today) resumes silently.
- [ ] Reloading after a swap shows no notice (current board is today's); the previous slot is unchanged.
- [ ] Fetch failure/timeout on the stale path falls back to resuming the old board with progress intact.
- [ ] Vitest covers every launch-decision branch (no save / today's daily / stale chip-chosen / stale auto-daily / ocr / manual / demo) and swap losslessness, reversibility, and chosen-explicitly marking; npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-i2h (board metadata + two-slot store)
close_reason: Implemented in d93ed80: launch decision + lossless swap/resume with transient notice; all acceptance criteria met, tests/lint/build green

comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** On page load with a stale auto-loaded daily board, load today's puzzle and offer a lossless, reversible resume of the previous board.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-i2h, boards carry `{ date?, source, chosenExplicitly }` metadata in the two-slot `{ current, previous? }` store, but a saved board still unconditionally resumes at launch — a player returning on a new day lands in yesterday's puzzle with no signal that today's exists.

**Desired behavior:**
The saved-puzzle store gains a launch decision, `decideLaunch(saved, todayISO)`, returning one of:
- `fetch-today` — no save exists (current auto-load behavior).
- `resume` — the current board is exempt, is a daily board dated today, or has `source: 'unknown'` (legacy; this branch is upgraded to a compare flow by connections-m80 — in this slice legacy boards resume silently, preserving current behavior).
- `fetch-swap` — the current board is a non-exempt daily board dated before today (ET).

**Exempt** means `chosenExplicitly === true` or `source` is `ocr`/`manual`/`demo`. This rule is shared by all later slices.

On `fetch-swap`: show the existing loading screen, fetch today's puzzle, and only on success atomically move the old board to `previous` and make today's board `current` (`source: 'daily'`, `chosenExplicitly: false`). Persisted state must not change before the fetch succeeds. The board then shows a dismissible, transient notice — draft copy "Loaded today's puzzle. [↩ Resume Monday's board] ✕", using the previous board's date label when known — rendered with theme tokens and a className for any animation. The notice is React state only, never persisted: reloading after a swap resumes today's board with no notice.

Tapping resume swaps `current ↔ previous` losslessly via a store function (`swapBoards`): the re-entered board becomes `current` with `chosenExplicitly: true` (exempting it from future auto-swaps); the outgoing board keeps its own metadata unchanged in `previous`, so swapping back later is equally lossless.

Fetch failure or timeout on the `fetch-swap` path falls back to resuming the old board untouched (the dedicated banner for this case ships in connections-apm). Skip during this loading screen keeps its current destination (the menu) this slice; connections-apm redirects it.

**Key interfaces:**
- `decideLaunch(saved, todayISO)` in the saved-puzzle store — the single decision point the app's launch effect switches on.
- `swapBoards(saved)` — pure swap with the chosen-explicitly marking described above.
- A function applying a successful daily fetch to the saved state for the swap case (old current → previous, fetched words/date → current).
- The app's launch effect and `loadToday` flow, which currently auto-load only when no save exists.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Returning on a later ET date with a non-exempt daily board lands on today's board with the notice visible and the old board in `previous`.
- [ ] Resume restores the old board with locks/labels intact and marks it `chosenExplicitly`; today's board, including sorting done before the tap, survives in `previous` and swapping back restores it.
- [ ] A resumed board does not auto-swap on later launches.
- [ ] Chip-chosen, OCR, manual, demo, legacy-`unknown`, and dated-today boards all resume silently with no notice.
- [ ] Reloading after a swap shows no notice.
- [ ] Fetch failure/timeout on the swap path resumes the old board with persisted state unchanged.
- [ ] Vitest covers every `decideLaunch` branch, the exemption rule, swap losslessness/reversibility/marking, and that failure paths leave state untouched. `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- The "Today's puzzle is ready" banner and Skip-path changes (connections-apm).
- The legacy word-compare flow (connections-m80) — legacy boards resume silently here.
- The menu backstop card (connections-vft) and refocus detection (connections-7a4).

title: Persist board metadata in a two-slot store and show the board date label
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

Introduce the saved-puzzle store as a pure, framework-free module owning a new persisted shape with two slots — current and previous — under the existing localStorage key. Every board records tiles, locked rows, and labels, plus new metadata: date (daily boards only, taken from the Worker response's resolved print date), source (daily / ocr / manual / demo), and a chosen-explicitly flag (true for past-date chip loads, false for auto-load and the "Today's Puzzle" card). Legacy `{tiles, lockedRows, labels}` blobs parse as a current board with unknown provenance and continue to resume exactly as today — no behavior change this slice. The board header shows a muted date label whenever the board has a date: "Today" for today's ET date, abbreviated weekday + date (e.g. "Fri, Jun 5") otherwise; dateless boards show no label.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Loading today's puzzle persists a current board stamped with the Worker-returned date, source "daily", and chosen-explicitly false; loading via a past-date chip stamps chosen-explicitly true.
- [ ] OCR, manual-entry, and demo loads persist their source accordingly, with no date.
- [ ] A pre-existing legacy saved blob still resumes on the board screen unchanged, and round-trips into the new two-slot shape with unknown provenance.
- [ ] Board header shows "Today" for a board dated today (ET), a weekday + date label for an older date, and no label for dateless boards.
- [ ] Vitest covers parse/serialize/migration (including malformed data yielding no save) and date-label formatting including the ET-midnight boundary; npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

None – can start immediately
close_reason: Implemented in 738e019: savedPuzzle store module + board date label; all acceptance criteria met, tests/lint/build green

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Introduce a two-slot saved-puzzle store with board metadata, and show a date label in the board header.

**Current behavior:**
The app persists one board to localStorage under the key `connections-puzzle` as a flat `{ tiles, lockedRows, labels }` blob. Nothing records which puzzle date the board holds or how it was loaded (daily fetch, screenshot OCR, manual entry, demo). The daily-puzzle fetch response from `GET /api/puzzle` already includes a server-resolved `date` field alongside `words`, but the client ignores it. The board screen gives no indication of which day's puzzle is displayed.

**Desired behavior:**
A new pure, framework-free module (the "saved-puzzle store", suggested name `savedPuzzle`) owns the persisted shape and all reasoning about it. The persisted value under the same `connections-puzzle` key becomes `{ current, previous? }` where each board is:

`{ tiles: string[16], lockedRows: boolean[4], labels: string[4], date?: 'YYYY-MM-DD', source: 'daily'|'ocr'|'manual'|'demo'|'unknown', chosenExplicitly: boolean }`

- Daily loads stamp `date` from the fetch response's `date` field (server-authoritative — do not stamp from the client clock), `source: 'daily'`, and `chosenExplicitly: false` for the auto-load and the "Today's Puzzle" card, `true` for the past-date chips.
- OCR, manual-entry, and demo loads stamp their `source` with no `date` and `chosenExplicitly: false`.
- A legacy flat blob parses into `{ current }` with `source: 'unknown'`, `chosenExplicitly: false`, no date. **This exact contract matters downstream: `source: 'unknown'` is how later slices recognize a legacy board.** Malformed data (wrong tile count, junk JSON) parses to "no save", as today.
- Launch behavior is otherwise unchanged this slice: any parseable save still resumes directly on the board.
- The board header shows a muted date label when `current.date` exists: `"Today"` when it equals today's ET date (the app already has a `todayET()` helper), otherwise an abbreviated weekday + month + day (e.g. "Fri, Jun 5"), formatted DST-safely from the ISO date parts the same way the existing recent-date chips build their labels. No label when the board has no date. Use theme tokens for color, never hex literals.

**Key interfaces:**
- The saved-puzzle store module: parse/serialize functions over the persisted shape, board constructors/stampers, and `dateLabel(dateISO, todayISO)` returning `'Today'`, a formatted label, or null.
- The daily-load path (currently `loadToday` in the app component) begins consuming the response's `date` field and passing source/chosen-explicitly through to persistence.
- The persist effect writes the new shape; the launch path reads via the store's parser.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Loading today's puzzle persists `current` with the Worker-returned date, `source: 'daily'`, `chosenExplicitly: false`; a past-date chip load persists `chosenExplicitly: true`.
- [ ] OCR, manual, and demo loads persist their respective `source` with no date.
- [ ] A legacy flat blob still resumes on the board screen with tiles/locks/labels intact, and is parsed as `source: 'unknown'`; malformed saves are treated as no save.
- [ ] Header shows "Today" for a board dated today (ET), a weekday + date label for older dates, and nothing for dateless boards.
- [ ] Vitest (no jsdom, mirroring the existing pure-module tests) covers parse/serialize/migration including malformed input, stamping, and `dateLabel` including the ET-midnight boundary. `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- Any launch-decision changes (auto-swap, notices, banners) — later slices (connections-4i5 onward).
- Writing the `previous` slot — nothing populates it yet this slice.
- Worker/API changes (the response already carries `date`).

title: PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle (new-day landing & new-puzzle awareness)
description:
## Problem Statement

When a player returns to the app on a new day, their saved board from a previous day wins at launch unconditionally. Nothing on screen says which day's puzzle it is or that a newer one exists, so players conclude the site hasn't updated and bounce. The saved state carries no date or source, so the app can't even distinguish a stale daily board from a deliberately-chosen older puzzle or a hand-entered one.

## Solution

Opening the app on a new day loads today's puzzle automatically — the same experience as a first visit — with a dismissible notice offering to resume the previous board. Players who deliberately chose an older puzzle, or who entered words by screenshot/typing/demo, keep resuming their board silently with no nagging. The board header always shows which day's puzzle it holds ("Today" / "Fri, Jun 5"). A tab left open overnight gets an unobtrusive "Today's puzzle is ready" banner rather than a content swap. Resume is a lossless swap between the current and previous boards, with a menu backstop entry.

## User Stories

1. As a returning daily player, I want today's puzzle to load automatically when I come back on a new day, so that I never mistake the app for stale and can start sorting immediately.
2. As a returning player with unfinished progress, I want a clear "resume previous board" option after today's puzzle loads, so that the auto-load never costs me my work.
3. As a player who deliberately chose an older puzzle or entered my own words, I want the app to resume my board silently, so that my intentional choice isn't nagged.
4. As a player, I want the board to show which day's puzzle I'm looking at, so that I can tell at a glance whether it's today's.
5. As a player who left the tab open overnight, I want a banner telling me today's puzzle is ready, so that I can switch with one tap without the board changing under me.
6. As an existing user with a board saved before this feature, I want my first launch after the update to keep my board if it's actually today's and otherwise load today's with resume offered, so that the fix reaches me without losing anything.
7. As a player who resumed a previous board, I want to swap back to today's board without losing progress on either, so that switching is safe to explore.

## Implementation Decisions

- Persisted state becomes two slots — current and previous — under the existing storage key. Each board carries tiles, locked rows, labels, plus new metadata: date (daily boards only), source (daily / ocr / manual / demo), and a chosen-explicitly flag.
- The puzzle date comes from the Worker response, which already returns the resolved print date; it is server-authoritative (no client-clock trust at save time).
- Exemption rule: a board is exempt from auto-swap and banners iff it was chosen explicitly (loaded via a past-date chip, or re-entered via resume) or has no trusted date (ocr/manual/demo).
- Page-load decision: no save → fetch today (current behavior). Current board is today's daily, or exempt → resume silently. Stale non-exempt daily → fetch today, move the old board to the previous slot, show the dismissible resume notice. Legacy metadata-less save → fetch today and set-compare words: equal → keep the board and stamp metadata; different → swap with notice; fetch failure → resume untouched.
- Fetch failure/timeout on the stale path falls back to the old board with a "Today's puzzle is ready" banner whose action retries. The loading screen's Skip on this path also goes to the old board with the banner, not the menu.
- Resume is a lossless swap of current ↔ previous; the resumed board is marked chosen-explicitly (exempt from future swaps). The menu shows a "Resume previous board" card whenever a previous board exists. One previous slot only — never an archive.
- The notice is transient (shown at swap time, not persisted across reloads); the menu card is the durable path back.
- Tab refocus (visibility change to visible): stale non-exempt board → show the banner; pure ET-date math, no fetch until tapped. A tab that stays visible across midnight gets nothing.
- Board header shows a muted date label whenever the board has a date: "Today" or weekday + date. Dateless boards show no label.
- One new pure, framework-free module (saved-puzzle store & launch policy) owns the schema, migration, launch decision, stamp/swap, exemption rules, and date labels behind a small interface; the app component remains the wiring layer.
- Word-set comparison is order-insensitive over the normalized uppercase words; daily words (including accents) pass through verbatim as they do today.
- No Worker/API changes.

## Testing Decisions

Vitest over the new pure module's external surface — inputs and returned state/decisions, no jsdom — mirroring how the worker puzzle module is tested:

- Migration: legacy blob parses into a current board with unknown provenance; malformed data yields no save.
- Launch decision branches: no save; today's daily; stale chip-chosen; stale auto-loaded daily; ocr/manual/demo; legacy.
- Applying a daily fetch: set-equal words stamp in place; differing words swap; word order and accented words handled.
- Swap: lossless, reversible, marks the re-entered board chosen-explicitly.
- Date labels: "Today", formatted older dates, null for dateless; ET-midnight boundary.

The app component wiring (notice, banner, visibility listener) remains untested, consistent with the repo's no-jsdom setup.

## Out of Scope

- Per-date save history or a board archive — one previous slot only (the app stays a word loader, per standing project constraint).
- jsdom / React Testing Library infrastructure for app-component tests.
- Interrupting an actively visible tab at midnight (deliberate: no nagging mid-play).
- Multi-tab localStorage synchronization (pre-existing behavior, unchanged).
- Changes to the recent-dates window or the Worker API.

## Further Notes

- The currently-bouncing cohort is exactly the set whose save never gets rewritten (they leave before loading anything new) — the legacy word-compare path is what reaches them on day one.
- Banner and notice should share one component/visual treatment.
- Draft copy — notice: "Loaded today's puzzle. [↩ Resume Monday's board] ✕"; banner: "Today's puzzle is ready — [Play today's puzzle] ✕". Final copy at implementation.

description: Tests landed in connections-29b but nothing runs them automatically — no CI, no hooks. A test that isn't run guards nothing, including the worker/puzzle.js privacy-contract suite. title: Test worker/index.js handler — routing, method guard, error mapping, edge caching
description: P1 follow-up to connections-29b. worker/index.js is untested. Cover: /api/puzzle GET routes to handler, other paths delegate to env.ASSETS.fetch; non-GET → 405 method_not_allowed; resolvePuzzleDate error → 400; PuzzleError → its .status/.code; non-PuzzleError throw → 502 fallback. Subtle/high-value: edge cache is keyed by resolved DATE not request URL, and ONLY 200s are cached (a transient 502 must not get stuck for an hour). Needs light globals — hand-roll Map-backed caches.default, ctx.waitUntil, env.ASSETS stubs (Vitest, no Workers pool unless cache semantics fight). Also assert prod handler and the vite.config.js dev middleware return the SAME status/body for the same inputs (405/400/PuzzleError mapping) so the two copies can't silently drift.

title: Auto-load today's NYT Connections words via Cloudflare Worker proxy (OCR/manual as fallback)
description:
OCR fails ~10% of the time (100% on some puzzles), causing users to bounce. Market feedback: people prefer helper sites (connections-copilot.com, connectionscompanion.com) that already have the day's words loaded. Research confirmed NYT publishes the puzzle at https://www.nytimes.com/svc/connections/v2/{YYYY-MM-DD}.json (verified live: no auth, returns categories[].cards[].content = 16 words + positions). The endpoint sends NO Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, so a browser fetch is CORS-blocked — a same-origin server hop is required. This app is already on Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets, so we add our own Worker proxy (exactly what connections-copilot.com does).

Make 'today's 16 words already loaded' the DEFAULT, keeping OCR + manual entry as fallback. To honor NYT's tolerance line (helpers OK; recreating/archiving the game not OK): the Worker strips category titles/groupings SERVER-SIDE and returns only the 16 words in board-position (display, scrambled) order — the answer key never reaches the browser. No solve mode, current-window only (today + last ~7 days, NOT a browsable archive), one puzzle per request.
design: Primary path: SPA GET /api/puzzle[?date=YYYY-MM-DD] -> Cloudflare Worker (run_worker_first: [/api/*]) fetches NYT v2 server-side, flattens categories[].cards by position, DISCARDS titles/groups, returns {date, words[16]} with short edge cache. Client maps through normalizeTileText -> existing loadPuzzle(words). Date window enforced server-side (today ET .. today-6, >= 2023-06-12 launch). Shared fetch+transform module reused by a Vite dev middleware so npm run dev works. Fallback chain: daily fetch -> OCR -> manual. UX: auto-load today when no saved puzzle (loading screen); resume + 'Today's Puzzle' menu card when a puzzle exists; recent-day chips; non-affiliation disclaimer + official-game link.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/puzzle returns {date, words:[16 uppercase strings]} and NEVER includes category titles/groupings; words are in board-position order. Date param validated to today..today-6 ET (and >= 2023-06-12); out-of-range/future -> error JSON, client falls back. No saved puzzle -> today auto-loads onto the board; saved puzzle -> resume + button to load today. Fetch failure/timeout -> graceful fall to OCR/manual, never a blank board or hard error. npm run dev serves /api/puzzle locally. npm run lint and npm run build pass.
notes: Implemented + verified on branch connections-r4m; PR #1 (https://github.com/bigintersmind/connections-sorter/pull/1). Worker proxy at /api/puzzle returns words-only (groupings stripped), date window today..today-6 ET enforced server-side, OCR/manual fallback intact. Close on merge.

title: UX review pass of the returning-user flow
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

A human review pass over the complete returning-user experience — this is the "we'll have to be careful so it's clear to the user" judgment call from the PRD, so the verification is "looks right," not a test suite. Review and adjust: notice and banner copy (finalize the draft copy); visual prominence vs. nag-factor; light and dark themes (CSS-var tokens only, no hex literals, per the repo's theming rules); reduced-motion behavior; keyboard reachability and screen-reader announcement of the notice/banner; rendering at narrow mobile widths; and an end-to-end gut check that deliberately-old players are genuinely not nagged.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every landing scenario demoed and signed off by a human: new-day auto-swap with notice, exempt silent resume (chip-chosen / OCR / manual / demo), fetch-failure banner, Skip-to-old-board path, refocus banner, legacy compare (both stamp and swap outcomes), and the menu backstop card.
- [ ] Notice and banner copy finalized; both render correctly in light and dark mode and at 375px width.
- [ ] prefers-reduced-motion is respected by any new animation; the notice/banner are keyboard-reachable and announced to assistive tech.
- [ ] Any copy/styling adjustments from the review are committed; npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-4i5 (auto-load + lossless resume)
- connections-vft (menu backstop card)
- connections-apm (failure/Skip banner)
- connections-m80 (legacy word-compare)
- connections-7a4 (refocus banner)

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Human UX review pass over the complete returning-user flow before it ships — copy, prominence, theming, accessibility, and the no-nag promise.

**Why ready-for-human, not ready-for-agent:**
The verification here is "looks and feels right" — there is no automatable acceptance signal. The PRD's explicit design concern was being careful that the new landing behavior is *clear to the user*; judging clarity, nag-factor, and visual balance requires human eyes on a real device. An agent may assist during the session (driving the browser, simulating dates/failures, applying agreed copy edits), but a human signs off.

**What to review:**
- Every landing scenario end-to-end: new-day auto-swap with resume notice; silent resume for chip-chosen / resumed / OCR / manual / demo boards; fetch-failure banner; Skip-to-old-board path; refocus banner; legacy compare (both stamp and swap outcomes); menu backstop card round-trip.
- Copy: finalize the draft notice ("Loaded today's puzzle…") and banner ("Today's puzzle is ready…") strings.
- Visual prominence vs. nag-factor: the notice/banner must be unmissable on first glance without feeling like an ad; the date label should recede.
- Theming: light and dark mode via CSS-var tokens only (no hex literals besides the four tile colors); check the banner/notice in both schemes.
- Motion: any new animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- Accessibility: notice and banner keyboard-reachable, dismiss buttons labeled, announcements via an appropriate live region (the loading screen already uses `role="status"` as prior art).
- Narrow viewport (~375px) rendering.
- Gut check: play as a deliberate old-puzzle player for a simulated day boundary and confirm nothing nags.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All scenarios above demoed and signed off by a human.
- [ ] Copy finalized and committed; banner/notice correct in light + dark at 375px.
- [ ] Reduced motion respected; keyboard and screen-reader paths verified.
- [ ] Any adjustments from the review are committed with `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` passing.

**Out of scope:**
- New features or scope changes discovered during review — file separate tickets.

title: Refocus banner for stale tabs (visibilitychange)
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

Cover returns that never reload the page (a desktop tab or surviving mobile tab left open overnight). On visibilitychange to visible, if the current board is a stale non-exempt daily — pure ET-date math, no network — show the "Today's puzzle is ready" banner from the failure-fallback slice. Tapping the action fetches today and swaps with the resume notice, same as the launch flow. A tab that stays continuously visible across midnight gets nothing (actively playing means no nag). Dismissing the banner hides it until the next refocus.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With a non-exempt daily board on screen, a hidden→visible transition after the ET date has advanced shows the banner, with no network request until the action is tapped.
- [ ] Exempt boards (chip-chosen, resumed, or dateless) never show the banner on refocus.
- [ ] No banner appears while the tab stays continuously visible across the ET midnight rollover.
- [ ] Dismissing the banner keeps it hidden until the next hidden→visible transition.
- [ ] npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-apm (banner component from the fetch-failure slice)

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Show the "Today's puzzle is ready" banner when a tab becomes visible again and the board on screen is now a stale non-exempt daily — covering returns that never reload the page.

**Current behavior:**
The launch decision only runs on page load. A tab left open overnight (desktop browser, or a mobile tab that survived) refocuses with yesterday's board mounted and no signal — the original bounce problem persists on that path.

**Desired behavior:**
On the document's `visibilitychange` event transitioning to visible, evaluate with pure ET-date math — no network request — whether the current board is a non-exempt daily board dated before today (same exemption rule as `decideLaunch`: not `chosenExplicitly`, `source` is `daily`). If so, show the banner from connections-apm on the board. Tapping the action runs the today-load and, on success, the standard swap + resume notice. Dismissing the banner hides it until the next hidden→visible transition. No interval timers: a tab that stays continuously visible across the ET midnight rollover shows nothing — the player is mid-session and must not be nagged. The listener must not fire fetches by itself; the only network activity is the user tapping the action.

**Key interfaces:**
- The staleness/exemption predicate in the saved-puzzle store (shared with `decideLaunch`) and the client `todayET()` helper.
- The banner component and retry flow from connections-apm — reused verbatim, parameterized only if copy differs.
- A `visibilitychange` listener in the app component, active while the board screen shows a daily board.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] With a non-exempt daily board on screen, a hidden→visible transition after the ET date advances shows the banner, with no request to `/api/puzzle` until the action is tapped.
- [ ] Tapping the action loads today and shows the resume notice; the old board is recoverable by resume.
- [ ] Exempt boards (chip-chosen, previously resumed, OCR/manual/demo) never show the banner on refocus.
- [ ] No banner appears while the tab stays continuously visible across ET midnight, and none on visibility transitions while the date hasn't changed.
- [ ] Dismissal lasts until the next hidden→visible transition.
- [ ] The staleness predicate is covered by Vitest (the listener itself is wiring, untested like the rest of the app component). `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- Auto-swapping content on refocus — the banner is deliberately the gentle path.
- Timers, polling, or service-worker/push mechanisms for midnight detection.

title: "Today's puzzle is ready" banner for fetch failure and the Skip path
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

A shared, dismissible banner shown on top of the board (draft copy: "Today's puzzle is ready — [Play today's puzzle] ✕"). Two entry points this slice: (1) when the stale-path launch fetch fails or times out, the user lands on their old board with the banner, and its action retries the today-load (on success: swap + resume notice, same flow as the launch path); (2) the loading screen's Skip on the stale path goes to the old board with the banner instead of the menu — launches with no save keep Skip → menu. The banner and the swap notice share one visual treatment, using theme tokens (no hex literals) per the repo's theming rules.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With requests to /api/puzzle failing and a stale non-exempt board saved, launch lands on the old board with the banner; tapping the action retries and, on success, swaps to today's board with the resume notice.
- [ ] During a stale-path load, Skip lands on the old board with the banner, not the menu; with no saved board, Skip still goes to the menu.
- [ ] The banner is dismissible and never appears on exempt boards (chip-chosen, resumed, or dateless).
- [ ] npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-4i5 (auto-load + lossless resume)

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** A dismissible "Today's puzzle is ready" banner on the board for the degraded paths: stale-launch fetch failure, and Skip during the stale-launch loading screen.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-4i5, a stale non-exempt launch whose fetch fails silently resumes the old board — the player is back in yesterday's puzzle with no signal. Skip during any loading screen goes to the menu, even when the player has a board they were working on.

**Desired behavior:**
A banner rendered at the top of the board screen — draft copy "Today's puzzle is ready — [Play today's puzzle] ✕" — sharing one visual treatment with the swap notice from connections-4i5 (ideally one component parameterized by copy/action; theme tokens only, classNames for hover/animation). Entry points this slice:

1. When the launch decision is `fetch-swap` (or `fetch-compare`, once connections-m80 lands) and the fetch fails or times out, the player lands on their old board with the banner shown.
2. Skip pressed during the loading screen of such a launch lands on the old board with the banner, instead of the menu. Launches with no saved board keep Skip → menu.

The banner's action re-runs the today-load; on success the normal swap happens and the resume notice appears. On another failure the existing fetch-error message behavior applies and the banner remains available. The ✕ dismisses the banner for the session (transient state, not persisted). Exempt boards (`chosenExplicitly`, or OCR/manual/demo source) never show the banner.

**Key interfaces:**
- The shared notice/banner component introduced alongside connections-4i5's notice.
- The launch effect's failure/timeout path and the loading screen's Skip handler — both need to know whether a saved board exists to fall back to.
- The existing `loadToday` flow for the retry action (it already handles aborts, timeout, and supersede semantics — reuse, don't duplicate).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] With `/api/puzzle` failing and a stale non-exempt daily board saved, launch lands on the old board with the banner; tapping the action with the network restored swaps to today's board with the resume notice.
- [ ] Skip during a stale-launch loading screen lands on the old board with the banner; with no saved board, Skip still goes to the menu.
- [ ] The banner is dismissible and never shown on exempt boards.
- [ ] Persisted state is unchanged by failure, Skip, or dismissal.
- [ ] `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- The refocus/visibilitychange trigger (connections-7a4) — it reuses this banner.
- Retry/backoff logic beyond the existing single-shot fetch with timeout.

title: Menu "Resume previous board" backstop card
description:
## Parent

connections-41d (PRD: Land returning users on today's puzzle)

## What to build

Whenever a previous board exists, the menu shows a "Resume previous board" card (with the previous board's date label when known, e.g. "Monday · 2 groups locked"). Tapping it swaps current ↔ previous and lands on the board screen. This is the durable path back after the transient swap notice has been dismissed — dismissing the notice must never strand the previous board.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After an auto-swap, dismissing the notice and opening the menu shows the resume card; tapping it restores the previous board with locks/labels intact and lands on the board screen.
- [ ] The card shows the previous board's date label when known, and does not render when no previous board exists.
- [ ] Returning to the menu and tapping the card again swaps back — lossless in both directions.
- [ ] npm test, lint, and build pass.

## Blocked by

- connections-4i5 (auto-load + lossless resume)

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Menu card to resume the previous board whenever one exists — the durable backstop after the transient swap notice is gone.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-4i5, a new-day auto-swap parks the old board in the `previous` slot and offers resume only via a transient notice. If the player dismisses the notice (or reloads), the previous board is still stored but unreachable from the UI.

**Desired behavior:**
The menu screen shows a "Resume previous board" card whenever the saved state has a `previous` board. The card uses the same visual language as the existing menu cards, shows the previous board's date label when known (via the store's `dateLabel`) and may show a small progress hint (e.g. locked-group count). Tapping it performs the same lossless `swapBoards` swap as the notice (marking the re-entered board `chosenExplicitly`) and lands on the board screen. Because the swap is symmetric, returning to the menu afterwards shows the card again — now pointing at the other board — so switching back and forth is always possible and never loses progress. No card renders when there is no `previous` board.

**Key interfaces:**
- The saved-puzzle store's `swapBoards` and `dateLabel` — reused, not reimplemented.
- The menu screen's card list; the new card should cancel any in-flight daily fetch before swapping, like the other menu actions do.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] After an auto-swap and dismissing the notice, the menu shows the card; tapping restores the previous board with locks/labels intact and lands on the board screen.
- [ ] The card shows the previous board's date label when it has one, and is absent when no previous board exists (fresh installs, never-swapped users).
- [ ] Swapping via the menu card twice round-trips both boards losslessly.
- [ ] `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.

**Out of scope:**
- Any additional history beyond the single `previous` slot (the app must not grow an archive).
- Changes to the notice or banner themselves.

title: Make AGENTS.md a symlink to CLAUDE.md (single source of truth)
description: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md had drifted (AGENTS.md falsely claimed no test runner, lacked the Worker proxy / theming / font sections). Consolidate into one canonical file (CLAUDE.md) and replace AGENTS.md with a committed symlink so both agent-doc paths stay byte-identical. Folded AGENTS.md's unique 'Non-Interactive Shell Commands' section into CLAUDE.md first to avoid losing content. Caveat: Windows clones without core.symlinks may see AGENTS.md as a text file containing 'CLAUDE.md'.
close_reason: Merged in PR #4 — AGENTS.md is now a committed symlink to CLAUDE.md

title: Match NYT tile text: shrink-to-fit font instead of mid-word line breaks
description: The official NYT Connections app never breaks a word mid-string — long single words (PENNSYLVANIA, PROTACTINIUM) shrink their font to fit one line, and multi-word entries (PUBLIC ADDRESS) wrap at the space. Our tiles use wordBreak:break-word (breaks inside words) plus a coarse length-based font ramp, producing splits like 'PENNSYLVAN/IA'. Replace with: wrap only at whitespace + a measurement-based auto-fit that steps the font down until each tile fits its square.
close_reason: Implemented shrink-to-fit tile font; verified in-browser at 390/320px — long single words shrink to one line, multi-word entries wrap at spaces, no grid overflow

title: Frontend design refresh: Libre Franklin type, board contrast, auto dark mode, subtle delight
description: Refine the UI (refined NYT-faithful direction). Fix accidental typography (no web font loads today; falls back to DIN/Helvetica inconsistently) by self-hosting Libre Franklin (NYT's actual family). Fix low board contrast (unlocked tiles #efefe6 on #fafaf5 bg). Introduce a CSS-variable theme layer to support automatic dark mode via prefers-color-scheme. Add subtle delight: staggered tile reveal, lifted selected state, satisfying lock, card/button hover states. Tighten spacing rhythm + row controls. MUST NOT regress: a11y (focus-visible, aria), SEO fallback, Tesseract self-hosting, puzzle contract, localStorage shape.
close_reason: Design refresh shipped: self-hosted Libre Franklin, CSS-variable theme layer with auto dark mode, fixed board contrast, subtle delight (staggered reveal, lifted selection, lock pop, hover lifts), a11y contrast bumps. Lint/build/tests green; verified across all 5 screens in light+dark.

title: Dedupe date helpers + constants + request handling across worker/client
description: P2 follow-up surfaced by the PR #1 test analysis. Three duplications can silently diverge: (1) addDays/todayET exist in BOTH worker/puzzle.js and src/App.jsx — note the client todayET takes no 'now' arg (harder to test); puzzle.js header says it's importable from either side, so the client could import instead of redeclaring. (2) PUZZLE_LAUNCH_DATE/RECENT_WINDOW_DAYS duplicated literals — drift makes chips offer dates the server then rejects. (3) Request/response handling reimplemented in worker/index.js AND vite.config.js (PuzzleError->status mapping by hand) — divergence yields bugs that only repro in one environment. Consider extracting a runtime-agnostic handlePuzzleRequest(method, dateParam)->{status,body} into puzzle.js that both adapt around. Either dedupe or add a shared parity test that pins both copies.
acceptance_criteria: date helpers + constants single-sourced (or pinned by a shared test); request handling shares one runtime-agnostic core or a parity test

title: Homepage menu revamp: explainer + preview + demo puzzle button
description: Replace the misplaced 'Tap two tiles to swap' hint on the menu with a real landing surface: tagline, 4x4 board preview graphic, third 'Try Demo Puzzle' button (hand-crafted sample), and 'How it works' 3-step section. Also tighten SEO meta/noscript copy and add semantic landmarks (main/nav/section, h1/h2). Lowers barrier to entry for new users and improves search-engine readability. Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/the-homepage-says-tap-fizzy-whale.md

title: Favicon: replace generic lightning glyph with Connections 4x4 grid
description: Current /public/favicon.svg is a generic purple lightning bolt with no connection to the site's theme or purpose. The OG image (public/og-image.png) already establishes the visual identity: a 4x4 grid of rounded tiles in the canonical Connections colors (yellow #f9df6d, green #a0c35a, blue #b0c4ef, purple #ba81c5), in solved order top→bottom. Make the favicon match — same grid, same colors, same order — so the tab icon instantly reads as 'Connections' and reinforces the brand established by the OG card.
close_reason: Replaced generic purple lightning SVG with a 4x4 grid in the canonical Connections colors (yellow, green, blue, purple, solved order top→bottom), matching the visual identity already established by public/og-image.png. Lint passes; renders cleanly at both 256px and 32px.

title: SEO improvements: prerender body, social card, robots/sitemap, canonical, JSON-LD, title tweak
description: Improve discoverability and link previews. Six changes: (1) Static H1+intro in #root so empty SPA shell stops hiding content from crawlers and social previews. (2) Add og:image + twitter:card=summary_large_image with a 1200x630 preview. (3) public/robots.txt + public/sitemap.xml so indexability is explicit. (4) <link rel=canonical> to lock the primary URL. (5) WebApplication JSON-LD in <head>. (6) Title includes 'NYT Connections' so the real search query has something to match.

title: Add horizontal tile padding so words don't touch the box edges
description: Follow-up to connections-atd. Official NYT tiles keep a clear margin between the word and the box sides; ours used only 3px horizontal padding, so shrunk long words sit nearly flush with the edge. Increase horizontal padding. fitTileFont measures clientWidth (which includes padding), so it auto-shrinks to respect the larger margin.
close_reason: Set horizontal tile padding to 5px — the ceiling that keeps a two-word tile (WET DOG) single-line down to 375px while giving long shrunk words more margin. Verified in-browser.

title: Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut on upload screen
description:
## Parent

connections-ynq

## What to build

A keyboard shortcut on the upload screen so desktop users can press Cmd/Ctrl+V to load a clipboard image into the preview without clicking the "Paste from clipboard" button. Reuses the clipboard image extractor from connections-ren; the listener routes paste-event clipboard data through the same downstream wiring (`pickFile()` on success, `setError()` on failure with the same three error messages).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] While the upload screen is mounted on a desktop browser (Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge) with an image on the clipboard, pressing Cmd+V (macOS) or Ctrl+V (Windows/Linux) renders that image in the preview area
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut works both when no image has been loaded yet and when a preview is already showing (in which case it replaces the current preview)
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut is a no-op while OCR is running (the `running` flag is true)
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image, the error message reads: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, the error message reads: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- [ ] When the paste event handler throws or otherwise errors, the error message reads: `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`
- [ ] The paste event listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts
- [ ] On screens other than the upload screen, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V has no behavior change related to image paste
- [ ] The button-click flow from connections-ren continues to work identically
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

## Blocked by

connections-ren (Paste-from-clipboard button on upload screen) — this slice imports the clipboard image extractor introduced in that ticket.
close_reason: Code complete in bc401ec. Cross-browser desktop verification gate is on the parent PRD (connections-ynq) and a human, per AFK contract.

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Add a Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut on the upload screen that loads a clipboard image into the preview, reusing the extractor from connections-ren.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-ren ships, users on the upload screen can load a clipboard image by clicking the new "Paste from clipboard" button. Pressing Cmd+V (macOS) or Ctrl+V (Windows/Linux) on the upload screen has no effect; desktop users must click the button.

**Desired behavior:**
While the upload screen is mounted, pressing Cmd+V or Ctrl+V loads any image on the clipboard into the upload screen's preview area, with the same downstream behavior as clicking the paste button. The shortcut works in both states: when no image has been loaded yet, and when a preview is already showing — in the latter case it replaces the current preview (this is non-destructive because OCR has not yet run). The shortcut is a no-op while OCR is in flight. The listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts; pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V on other screens has no related effect.

**Key interfaces:**

- **Clipboard image extractor** — Already shipped in connections-ren. The paste-event listener takes `clipboardData.items` from the `ClipboardEvent`, passes them through this extractor, and routes the resulting discriminated union to the same downstream calls used by the button click (preview-loading helper on `ok`, error-message state on `no-image` / `unsupported-format` / `error`).

- **Upload screen component** — The existing React component (post-connections-ren). The `paste` event listener is added via a `useEffect` whose lifetime matches the component. The listener checks the in-flight OCR flag (already present in component state) and is a no-op while OCR is running.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] While the upload screen is mounted on a desktop browser (Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge) with an image on the clipboard, pressing Cmd+V or Ctrl+V renders that image in the preview area
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut works both when no image has been loaded yet and when a preview is already showing — in the latter case the new image replaces the existing preview
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut is a no-op while OCR is running
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V surfaces the error message exactly: `No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first.`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V surfaces exactly: `That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker.`
- [ ] When the paste-event handler errors (or the extractor returns `{ kind: "error" }`), the message is exactly: `Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead.`
- [ ] The paste event listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts (no listener leaks across screens)
- [ ] On screens other than the upload screen, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V has no behavior change related to image paste
- [ ] The button-click flow from connections-ren continues to work identically
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

**Out of scope:**

- The button-click entry path — already shipped in connections-ren
- Reimplementing the extractor — reuse the one introduced in connections-ren
- Tests
- Mobile-specific paste behavior — iOS Safari fires `paste` events only inside editable elements, so this listener will be a desktop-only path in practice; this is expected and documented in the parent PRD

**Verification gate before merge:**
The parent PRD requires manual cross-browser verification on desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge before close-out. The agent's responsibility ends at "code complete, `npm run lint` passing, PR opened". A human performs the cross-browser check before merge.

title: Paste-from-clipboard button on upload screen
description:
## Parent

connections-ynq

## What to build

A new "Paste from clipboard" peer button on the upload screen, alongside the existing "Choose an image" button. When clicked, it reads any image on the user's clipboard via the Async Clipboard API, loads it into the existing preview, and routes through the existing `pickFile()` and OCR flow. Includes the underlying clipboard image extractor module that decides what's an acceptable image and what error to surface.

When the browser doesn't support clipboard image reads, the button is hidden entirely — no visual change to users on those browsers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] On the upload screen, a "Paste from clipboard" button is visible when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` is true
- [ ] On browsers where that capability check is false, the paste button is not rendered, and the screen falls back to the existing single-button layout
- [ ] The paste button uses the same dropzone-card visual style as "Choose an image": clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied"
- [ ] The paste button is stacked vertically below the existing "Choose an image" button
- [ ] On iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard, clicking the paste button renders the image in the preview area
- [ ] On desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge with an image in the clipboard, clicking the paste button renders the image in the preview area
- [ ] After a successful paste, clicking "Extract Words" runs OCR and produces the same output as uploading the same image via the file picker
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image (empty, or text only), the error message reads: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, the error message reads: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- [ ] When `navigator.clipboard.read()` throws (permission denied, etc.), the error message reads: `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`
- [ ] The clipboard image extractor is implemented as a pure function returning a discriminated union with kinds `ok`, `no-image`, `unsupported-format`, `error` — accepting `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`; rejecting `image/heic`, `image/heif`, `image/svg+xml` with `unsupported-format`
- [ ] The existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

## Blocked by

None – can start immediately.

title: PRD: Paste screenshot from clipboard on upload screen
description:
## Problem Statement

On iOS 16+, users can save a screenshot directly to the clipboard from the screenshot toolbar instead of saving it to Photos. For users who use this flow as their default, the Connections puzzle app currently has no way to accept a clipboard image — the only entry point is the file picker, which routes through Photos. These users have to save the screenshot to Photos first (an extra step) or abandon the screenshot path entirely.

Desktop users hit the same gap from the other direction: they expect Cmd/Ctrl+V to "just work" when an image is on the clipboard, but the upload screen ignores paste events.

## Solution

Add a "Paste from clipboard" affordance to the upload screen as a first-class peer to the existing "Choose an image" button. On iOS, the affordance is a tap target. On desktop, the same Blob can also arrive via Cmd/Ctrl+V. Either way, the pasted image flows into the existing OCR pipeline — same preview, same "Extract Words" confirmation, same output.

## User Stories

1. As an iOS user who copied a Connections screenshot using the screenshot toolbar's "Copy" button, I want to paste it directly into the upload screen, so that I don't have to save to Photos first.
2. As a desktop user with a screenshot on my clipboard, I want to press Cmd/Ctrl+V on the upload screen and have my screenshot loaded, so that the interaction matches my muscle memory from every other web app.
3. As a user on a browser that doesn't support clipboard image reads, I want the upload screen to look unchanged, so that I'm not shown a feature I can't use.
4. As a user whose clipboard has the wrong contents (empty, text-only, or an unsupported format), I want a clear and specific error message, so that I know what to do next instead of seeing a generic OCR failure later.
5. As a user who pasted the wrong image, I want to paste a different one without having to navigate away first, so that recovering from a clipboard mistake is fast.

## Implementation Decisions

### Module decomposition

- **Clipboard image extractor** — A pure function that accepts heterogeneous clipboard data (either the `ClipboardItem[]` returned by `navigator.clipboard.read()` or the `DataTransferItem[]` from a `paste` event's `clipboardData.items`) and returns a discriminated union with one of four kinds: `ok` (carries the Blob), `no-image` (no image-typed entries at all), `unsupported-format` (only HEIC/HEIF/SVG present), or `error` (anything else). Encapsulates the MIME type policy, the unification of the two API shapes, and the precedence rule (first accepted item wins when multiple images are present).
- **Clipboard capability check** — A pure helper returning whether `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` are both truthy. Used to gate rendering of the paste button.
- **UploadScreen integration** — Modifications to the existing upload component: conditional render of the paste button, click handler that calls `navigator.clipboard.read()` and routes the result through the extractor, a paste-event listener attached via `useEffect` while the component is mounted, and downstream wiring into the existing `pickFile()` and `setError()` paths. The OCR pipeline itself is unchanged.

### MIME type policy

- Accepted: `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`.
- Explicitly rejected with a specific error: `image/heic`, `image/heif`, `image/svg+xml`.
- Anything else: treated as no-image-found.

### UI

- The paste button is a peer to "Choose an image", stacked vertically below it, using the same dropzone-card visual style: clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied".
- When the capability check returns false, the paste button is hidden entirely. The layout collapses to the existing single-button look — no visual regression in unsupported environments.

### Two entry paths, one downstream behavior

- Button click: `navigator.clipboard.read()` → extractor → either `pickFile(blob)` or `setError(message)`.
- Cmd/Ctrl+V: `paste` event listener inspects `event.clipboardData.items` → extractor → same downstream wiring. Listener is mounted while the upload screen is mounted. No-op while OCR is running. Works in both the empty and preview states (re-pasting replaces the current preview, which is non-destructive because OCR hasn't run yet).

### Error messages (reuse existing `setError` UI)

- `no-image` from extractor: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- `unsupported-format` from extractor: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- `error` from extractor, plus any thrown exception from `clipboard.read()` (permission denied, unknown errors): `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`

### Flow consistency

- After a successful paste, the preview renders and the user must click "Extract Words" — identical to the file-picker flow. No auto-OCR. The clipboard might hold a stale screenshot from earlier; the preview is the safety net.

## Testing Decisions

No tests are being added as part of this work. The project has no test runner configured today, and the MIME policy is short enough to eyeball-verify in code review. A real-hardware acceptance check on iOS Safari (see acceptance criteria below) covers the highest-stakes failure mode.

## Acceptance criteria

- Paste button visible on the upload screen when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` is true; hidden otherwise.
- On iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard: clicking the paste button loads the image into the preview, and clicking "Extract Words" produces the same OCR output as the file-picker flow on the same image.
- On desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge: both the button click and Cmd/Ctrl+V load the clipboard image into the preview.
- Empty clipboard, text-only clipboard, HEIC-only clipboard, and permission-denied each surface the matching error message.
- Existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged.
- Verified on real iOS hardware before close-out — particularly that `image/png` is what arrives from the screenshot toolbar's "Copy", and that the HEIC error path actually triggers (or that iOS Safari converts to PNG transparently and HEIC never appears).

## Out of Scope

- Telemetry or analytics on which entry path users prefer. The app has no telemetry today; introducing it is its own decision.
- A separate "Paste a different image" button in the preview state. Cmd/Ctrl+V covers re-entry; a button for an action almost no user will take twice in a row would add visual weight without payoff.
- HEIC support via a third-party decoder. iOS Safari typically gives PNG from the screenshot toolbar, so HEIC is a corner case (Photos copy, not screenshot copy). A specific error message is sufficient.
- Setting up a test runner. Considered and explicitly declined — see Testing Decisions.

## Further Notes

- iOS Safari only fires `paste` events inside editable elements, so the keyboard listener is effectively a no-op there. iOS users go through the button. The listener is a desktop-only path in practice.
- Once iOS records a "Don't Allow" response to its Allow Paste dialog, subsequent reads in the same session fail with `NotAllowedError`. The generic error message gives users a path forward without trying to re-prompt.

Follow-up review hardening landed in e1e8919 (target check on window paste listener, devtools logging at the four catch sites, comment rewrite). Manual hardware verification per acceptance criteria remains the user's call but the code is in main.

title: Paste screenshot from clipboard on upload screen
description: On iOS 16+, users can save a screenshot directly to the clipboard instead of Photos, but the app currently has no way to accept that — the only entry point is the file picker. Add a 'Paste from clipboard' affordance on the upload screen so a clipboard image can be fed into the existing OCR pipeline. Desktop users (Cmd/Ctrl+V) benefit too.
design:
Add a 'Paste from clipboard' button alongside the existing 'Choose an image' button on UploadScreen (src/App.jsx:546). On click, call navigator.clipboard.read(), iterate ClipboardItems, find one whose types include 'image/png' or 'image/jpeg', call getType() to get a Blob, and pass it to the existing pickFile() at src/App.jsx:469 — no changes to the OCR pipeline needed since pickFile already handles any image Blob/File.

Feature-detect navigator.clipboard?.read and hide the button if unsupported (older browsers, non-HTTPS contexts).

Reuse setError for the failure modes: permission denied, no image on clipboard, unsupported MIME (e.g. HEIC if Tesseract can't read it — verify before shipping).

Optional polish: also attach a 'paste' event listener on the upload screen container so desktop users can Cmd/Ctrl+V without clicking the button. iOS won't fire this reliably outside editable elements, so the button is the load-bearing path.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'Paste from clipboard' button visible on upload screen when navigator.clipboard.read is available
- Clicking it on iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard loads the image into the preview and runs OCR identically to file upload
- Clicking it on desktop Chrome/Safari/Firefox/Edge does the same
- Permission denied, empty clipboard, and non-image clipboard contents all surface a clear error via the existing error UI
- Button is hidden (not just disabled) on browsers without clipboard image read support
- Existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged
notes: Context: iOS 16+ added 'Copy' alongside 'Save to Photos' in the screenshot toolbar, and many users now use it as their default. The Async Clipboard API requires HTTPS (we have it via Cloudflare Workers) and a user gesture (the button click satisfies this). iOS shows a one-time 'Allow Paste' permission prompt — that's OS behavior, not something to engineer around.
